mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive during months of positive PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate is held above 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy increases borrowing costs and reduces equity risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal a contraction in both supply and final demand that is deeper than either variable alone suggests, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of INDPRO and RSAFS does not exhibit a significantly lower mean 20-day forward return than either variable in isolation (pairwise t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals an impending output contraction that triggers broad de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth is not associated with significantly negative 60-day S&P 500 returns during periods of falling unemployment and above-median capacity utilization over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,500 thousand units, the residential construction sector is in contraction, dragging down construction employment and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1,500 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a stagflationary risk premium where restrictive policy amplifies labor-market distress, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of UNRATE change and FEDFUNDS level does not add significant explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) for 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly captures nominal consumer expenditure momentum, which drives corporate top-line expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth does not coincide with positive mean 5-day S&P 500 forward returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) rises at an annualized pace above 3.0%, elevated inflation pressures compress real earnings and force tighter monetary policy expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative during months when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0% (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
